observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant  and  the
        City Treasurer to:


        a. initiate the request for write-off of dormant items in the Other
           Receivables account which were not properly supported with valid
           documents in accordance  with  the  procedures  set-out  in  COA
           Circular No. 2016-005; and

        b. conduct a thorough analysis on  the  Other  Receivable  accounts
           with available documents  to  identify  the  debtors  and  issue
           demand letters.

2. We recommended that Management requires the:


        a.  Acting  General   Services   Officer/Inventory   Committee   to
           consistently conduct physical count of  inventories  and  submit
           the RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year  for
           the first and second semester, respectively;

        b. Designated Supply Custodian/Acting City General Services Officer
           to maintain complete stock cards for each type of inventory; and

        c. City Accountant and Acting  City  General  Services  Officer  to
           reconcile their  respective  inventory  records  to  affirm  the
           accuracy of the reported balances of the Inventory  accounts  in
           the Statement of Financial Position.

3. We recommended that Management:


        a. Require the GSO and OCA to exhaust  all  means  to  resolve  the
           discrepancies noted above and effect the  necessary  adjustments
           on   the   affected    accounts.    Moreover,    complete    the
           information/details lacking including  values  of  land  in the
           respective reports;


        b.  Require  the  OCA  to  maintain  the  RPLC  to  facilitate  the
           reconciliation of records;


        c. Require the GSO to prepare PCs to all lots recorded per property
           with complete information  needed  such  as  acquisition  dates,
           cost, and description;
        d. Require the  CLO,  CAO  and  GSO  to  initiate  the  process  of
           transferring the respective TCTs in the City’s  name  to  secure
           the ownership of properties and investigate the  TCTs  for  lots
           without supporting TCTs; and

        e. Strictly comply with provisions of Paragraphs 15 and 18 of IPSAS
           1; Paragraphs 26 and 27 of IPSAS 17; Article 47 (c) of Rule  IX,
           of Local Government Code of 1991; Annexes 11 and 14,  Volume  II
           of the MNGAS Manual for LGUs; and Section 140  and  148  of  COA
           Circular No. 92-386.

4. We recommended that Management:


        a. Require the GSO and OCA to:


            • Exhaust all means to resolve the  discrepancies  noted  above
              and effect the necessary adjustments;


            • Prepare LRNPC and  LRNLC,  respectively,  to  facilitate  the
              reconciliation of records; and


            • Strictly comply with Item 2, Accounting Policies; and Annexes
              D and E of COA Circular No. 2015-008; Paragraphs 3.7 and  3.8
              of COA Circular No. 2016-004; and Sec. 491, Volume I and
              Section 53, Volume III of GAAM.


        b. Require the OCA to:


            • Include in the Notes to Financial Statements  the  disclosure
              on Road Networks as prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular
              No. 2015-008; and


            • Strictly comply with Paragraph 27 of  IPSAS  1  and  Item  2,
              Reporting Guidelines of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

6. We recommended that Management require the OCA to:


        a. Analyze discrepancies and effect the  necessary  adjustments  on
           the affected accounts;


        b.   Identify   appropriately   those   PPE   with   no    specific
           details/description  for  proper  computation   of   accumulated
           depreciation; and

        c. Strictly comply with Paragraph 42 of IPSAS 3; Paragraphs 13, 67,
           and 71 of IPSAS 17; and Paragraph 14, Accounting Policies of COA
           Circular No. 2015-008.

7. We recommended that  Management  require  the  City  Accountant  to
        review and   analyze the accounts which had  been  outstanding  for
        two years or more and no actual claim, administrative  or  judicial
        has been filed.  If upon review, the conditions for reversion exist
        and merit removal from the book of  accounts,  the  same  shall  be
        disclosed in the FS.

1. We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to:

        a. Coordinate with the Accounting Office of  the  concerned  source
           agency to request the details of the NGAs  receivable  from  the
           City to verify the actual balance of the funds  transferred  and
           thereafter reconcile any discrepancy.

        b. Request written  approval  from  source  agencies  so  that  the
           unexpended balance may be transferred to the GF pursuant to  COA
           Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2014.

        c.  Reclassify  Due  to  NGAs  fund  transfers  made  by  Corporate
           Government Agencies amounting to ₱273,776.00  to  Due  to  GOCCs
           account, to correct the original entry.

1. We recommended that  Management  direct  the  City  Engineer,  City
        Planning Development Officer and City Budget  Officer  to  set/plan
        its procurement for the projects  funded  by  the  20%  DF  at  the
        earliest time and fast track the implementation of all  development
        projects programmed in the Annual Investment Program in  accordance
        with DILG-DBM-DOF JMC No. 1 for the attainment of the desired socio-
        economic development of the City.

1. We recommended that Management to instruct the DRRMO to prepare and
        submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the  LDRRMF  to
        COA within the required time frame under Section 4, Item 25 of RA
        No. 10121 and Section 5.1.5 of  COA  Circular  No.  2012-002  dated
        September 12, 2012 so that validation and monitoring of the various
        programs, projects and activities funded by the LDRRMF can be  done
        timely.

1. We recommended that Management direct the  DRRMO,  in  coordination
        with LDRRMC, City Planning and Development  Coordinator  and  Local
        Finance Committee to strictly comply  with  Section  5.1.2  of  COA
        Circular No. 2012-002 on the preparation of LDRRMFIP ensuring  that
        the projects and activities included therein include not  only  the
        allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness for
        a particular year but also the total accumulated unexpended  LDRRMF
        from previous years.

1. We recommended that Management direct the City DRRM Officer and the
        City Engineer to:

        a. ensure that all budgeted programs and projects were carried  out
           accordingly to optimize utilization of funds;


        b. see to it that priority programs and projects funding  of  which
           can be sourced from the balance of the continuing appropriations
           and previous years’ balances transferred to Special  Trust  Fund
           are included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to fully utilize  all
           available sources of funds; and


        c. prepare monitoring report  to  properly  tract  and  assess  the
           implementation of all LDRRM programs, projects and activities.

1. We recommended that Management instruct the GFPS to:


          a.  Strictly  observe  the  timelines  in  the  preparation   and
             submission of GAD  Plan  and  Budget  and  GAD  Accomplishment
             Report pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 and  COA
             Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and


          b.  Submit  the  updated/revised  GAD  Plan  and  Budget  to  the
             concerned DILG Office for information and  monitoring  of  the
             revised programs and projects.

1. We recommended that GFPS take the following courses of action:


          a. Prioritize programs and projects that will effectively  address
             gender issues before inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget;


          b. Ensure that programs and projects lined-up  for  implementation
             during the year are fully carried out as planned; and


          c. Ensure that expenditures are within the approved GAD  Plan  and
             Budget.

2. We recommended that Management direct the GSO to submit copies of
           all approved Contracts and POs together  with  duly  accomplished
           supporting documents not later than five (5)  working  days  from
           its execution. Also, notify the  Auditor  within  24  hours  upon
           acceptance of the deliveries in compliance with Items  3.1.2  and
           3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Item A.2 of  COA  Circular
           96-010.

3. We recommended that Management instruct the  City  Accountant  to
           require the PRC to submit the liquidation  report  detailing  the
           actual utilization of funds paid in advance for COVID19 testing.

4. We recommended that Management instruct:


          a. The OCA to:


             • Remit the full amount of taxes  withheld  during  the  month
               within  the  prescribed  period  to  avoid   incurrence   of
               unnecessary interests, surcharges and penalties;


             • Exert effort in identifying  previous  years’  balances  and
               make necessary adjustment  and  reconciliations  thereof  to
               facilitate immediate remittance of the same  to  BIR,  GSIS,
               Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; and


             •   Enhance   internal   controls   by   conducting    regular
               reconciliation of amounts withheld  and  remitted  to  avoid
               recurrence of over or under remittance.


          b. The Human Resource Management Office to:


             • Update the BIR,  GSIS,  Pag-IBIG  and  PhilHealth  with  any
               subsequent  changes,  as  required   by   their   respective
               policies, and do follow-ups if these changes  were  effected
               and implemented to avoid incurrence  of  unmatched  records;
               and


             • Verify/review the accuracy of the amounts to be remitted  to
               BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to  avoid  incurrence  of
               over or under remittance.

City Treasurer- Send written notices to the payees one month before the check becomes stale as required in Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs;

Henceforth, strictly observe Section 59 Chapter 3 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I, on proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale and cancelled checks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Submit the APP and the corresponding PPMP to this Office for audit and evaluation; and
Strictly comply with the requirements of Section 7 and Section 3.2, Appendix 2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Copies of Revised APP and PPMPs were submitted to COA by the City Budget Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require :

The OCA to stop the practice of processing payments without complete supporting documents;
The GSO and the OCA to submit the documents, enumerated under Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and Sections 23 and 25 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to the COA for verification and audit; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Items 9.1 to 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Sections 23 and 25 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding submission of copies of government contracts, purchase orders and all of the necessary supporting documents to the COA.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
All paid disbursements are supported with complete necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

Direct the LDRRMC/LDRRM Officer to monitor and evaluate the implementation of all DRRM PPAs to achieve the benefits fully desired to be derived therefrom in compliance with the provisions of Section 1, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA No. 10121; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of the RA No. 10121 and its IRR and the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC, DBM and DILG) Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in the implementation of LDRRMF PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Management submitted a copy of the revised LDRRMFIP showing the re-aligned COVID19 PPAs. They also gave justifications/ explanations on the observations noted.

observations.recommendation:

Submit a revised 20% DFAIP showing the re-aligned COVID-19 related programs/projects/activities
Direct the City Development Council/City Planning & Development Officer to monitor and evaluate the implementation of all 20% DF programs, projects and activities to achieve the benefits fully desired to be derived therefrom in compliance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Supplemental AIP prepared and approved by the Sanggunian Panglunsod for the realigned PPAs for COVID-19 response was submitted.
The Planning Office in coordination with the City Budget Office is monitoring the accomplishment and utilization of the expenses charged to the 20% development fund, the corresponding utilization report was submitted quarterly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require the GAD Office and Implementing Departments/Offices to ensure that GAD PPAs are implemented as planned, and monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs to fully maximize the potential benefits that the intended beneficiaries can derive therefrom; and
Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system for the effective and efficient implementation of GAD plans and programs; and prioritize the PPAs in the preparation of the GPB following the actual and/or requirements needed to preclude over estimation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Implemented
Reiterated in AAR CY 2021, Observation No. 14, page No. 98
The GFPS conducted its semestral meeting for Monitoring and Evaluation where GAD Proponents reported the status of implementation of their PPAs. The GAD monitoring and evaluation system was established as one of the monitoring tool in the preparation of the GPB and implementation of the GAD PPAs.
observations.reason: The GFPS in coordination with the City Budget Office is monitoring the accomplishment and utilization of the GAD expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Local School Board consider the budget of the City Division Office, DepEd, in the preparation of the SEF budget, taking into consideration the prior years' experience and the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations, Disbursements and Balances of the Division of Las Pinas;
The Local School Board, in case of Supplemental Budget, require a certification from the DepEd Accountant that additional appropriation of funds for the expenditure items chargeable against SEF should be net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council, and none is available from the City Division Office budget; and
The City Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to effect the necessary adjustments of the erroneous recording and misclassification of accounts; and strictly comply with the provisions of Item 4.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, series of 2017.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Salaries and other personnel benefits of non-teaching personnel assigned at DepEd Division Office were included in the General Fund Budget.
Salaries and other personnel benefits of non-teaching personnel assigned at DepEd Division Office were included in the General Fund Budget.

Regularly perform the necessary physical inventory and periodic reconciliation of balances to ensure that the OCA and the GSO records are accurately and reliably maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to require the concerned officers and employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances immediately.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Cash advances were fully liquidated by the concerned Accountable Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management Conduct actual physical count of supplies and materials regularly to ensure control, monitoring and accounting of all inventory items and establish the ending balances of inventories account in the financial statements as of year-end.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Inventory Committee was created by the City who will take charge of the inventory count of supplies and materials. The members of the Inventory Committee includes the GSO and the Accounting Office to ensure control and monitoring of all items and accurately report the balances in the City’s financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that succeeding appropriation ordinances shall be submitted to the DBM within three (3) days after their approval in compliance with the provisions of Sections 56 and 326 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
The City’s 2021 Annual Budget was approved on January 8, 2021 (Friday) then submitted/endorsed to the DBM on January 12, 2021. This was received by the said Office on January 14, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

Review and ensure that all entries in the Personnel Schedule, such as the name of personnel, the position title, the salary grade and step, and the amount of annual salary, are accurate before the approval of the City Mayor, maintain an updated copy thereof, and submit the same to the City Budget Office before the preparation of the Annual Budget; and
Evaluate the accuracy of the Personnel Schedule supporting the Appropriation Ordinance to establish the correct amount of PS in the approved Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
The City Personnel Office and the City Budget Office reviewed and reclassified in the recent Personnel Schedule position titles and its corresponding salary grades. Position titles and salary grades were already corrected and updated in the Personnel Schedule.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

Continuously review the existing human resources complement and consider filling up the vacant plantilla positions to maximize the available appropriations for the Personal Services account and to augment the workforce for the achievement of the City’s goals and objectives; and
Re-evaluate the existing policies in the hiring of emergency, casual and contractual employees to minimize the expenditures charged to the MOOE.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Implemented
On going review and updating of the existing human resources complement. The City Personnel Office is still reviewing the existing human resources complement of the City. The City Personnel Office had requested all departments to review and give recommendations on their existing plantilla positions. On going review and updating of the existing policies.
observations.reason: The City Personnel Office is still reviewing department manpower complement, organizational structure and their duties and functions.

The GSO require the different City Departments/ Offices to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials, by type, as required under Section 124 of the same Manual.
The GSO and Accounting Office oversees the conduct of the inventory process regularly to ensure control, monitoring and accounting of all inventory items and accurately report the balances as presented in the City’s financial statements.

Fully Implemented
Inventory Committee was created by the City who will take charge of the inventory count of supplies and materials. The members of the Inventory Committee includes the GSO and the Accounting Office to ensure control and monitoring of all items and accurately report the balances in the City’s financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management sees to it that a complete inventory report is submitted to the GSIS annually for the appraisal of insurance premiums due to the City to ensure that all property is covered with insurance to protect its interest in case of loss or damage on assets brought about by unforeseen events.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Implementations: Inventory report of buildings owned by the City were prepared by the GSO at the City Hall compound are insured with the Government Service Insurance System.
observations.reason: The total value of properties reported in the GF and SEF books were not adequately insured for CY 2019. The General Services Office is in coordination with the Assessor’s Office, Engineering Office and the Government Service Insurance System to determine the actual value of other City-owned buildings and have them insured with the latter.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that Management exerts further effort by identifying the nature of the account and coordinate with the depository bank to make the necessary adjustments in order to correct the balance of the cash account as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Management coordinated with the depository bank and disclosed that no account existed relative to the recorded P8,217.37 in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management through the City Accountant:

Verify the account balances especially the lot amortization collected by the City for the NHA housing project in Barangay CAA and those unremitted loan amortization of borrowers collected by the City for DSWD for project-SEAP and coordinate with the concerned government agencies regarding the individual loan profile of beneficiaries for remittance of said collections to concerned agencies; 
Confirm the balances with the agencies concerned. In case of positive confirmation, reconcile the records and make adjustments, if necessary; and
Based on the result of confirmation, revert the unliquidated amount to the GF.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Reiterated in AAR CY 2021, Observation No. 8, page No. 84
The City Accountant will validate existence of the claims and coordinate with concerned government agencies regarding the individual loan profile of beneficiaries for remittance of collections. No confirmation was made by management as of December 31, 2021. Instead the City Accountant reclassified the fund transfer from NHA to Due to GOCCs account through JEV No. 00465.
observations.reason: The balance of the Due to NGA-NHA as of December 31, 2021, amounted to P2,023,719.21. No reversion was made for CY 2021, instead the City Accountant reclassified the fund transfer from NHA to Due to GOCCs account through JEV No. 00465.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to coordinate with their counterparts at Pasay City and the concerned banks to reconcile account balances and ultimately transfer said cash balances to the City Government of Las Piñas. The City Accountant also coordinate with the Accountant of MMDA in order to reconcile the account balances and to effect the reclassification of the account to the proper Due from NGAs- MMDA.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Coordinated with Pasay City several times, but the concerned officials were not available. They shall set an appointment again with the concerned officials. Due from LGUs account was reclassified to Account Due from NGAs-MMDA per JEV No. 15180 dated 12/29/2016.
observations.reason: Appointment with concerned officials will still be made. The Due from LGUs account was reclassified to Account Due from NGAs-MMDA per JEV No.15180 dated 12/29/2016.
